entry.title: 1. Week of April 26th, 2026
entry.summary: «Hello, welcome to my first weeknote. I feel tired in a good way and I have been working so much that I never even had the chance to sit in melancholy by the window and watch the first pre-monsoon…»

entry.title: [Weeknotes] Week of Feb 14th, 2026
entry.summary: «My voice felt better, but my body was clearly still fighting something off. I felt lethargic and annoyed. I spent three separate days rotting, which is a lot, and goes way beyond my once-a-month…»

entry.summary: «Woah! I knew I’d skipped two weeknotes but I didn’t realise my last one was from last year. Yes, I was dying and it was a cause for alarm, but the chaos was planned for (as much as one is able to,…»

entry.title: Weeknote 2026-W3 | IQ vs AQI
entry.summary: «I missed writing the previous weeknote so this for both Week 2 and Week 3. I realised that most of the time I’m in ‘doing’ mode and the weeknotes seem to be giving me the time to pause and reflect on…»

entry.summary: «Ah another week blurred right past me. A rather emotionally exhausting one.»

entry.summary: «It's definitely been longer than a week, but even the 7-day week felt like a year. Things happen so fast and it all gets so foggy and muddled. I don't know if I have it in me to write a day-wise…»

entry.title: Weeknote 2026-W1 | '12 grapes under the table'
entry.summary: «I’m not a new year resolutions person, but there have been some resolutions that I had which have nicely rolled over to 2026 and life just happens to be in the right spot for me to act on them. One…»

entry.title: Week Note 1
entry.summary: «First time writing a week note. Don't know how this differs from a journal entry. I guess in the sense that it is 'supposed' to be more a description of events of the week as opposed to an amorphous…»

entry.title: Weeknote #14 [W26.01] - Blocking people, moral questions, and coconut water coffee
entry.summary: «I’ve lately been getting unprompted barrages of abusive messages from someone I used to (and still do) care about. I have no idea why they are doing this, nor do I know how to respond.»

entry.title: [Weeknotes] Week of Dec 27th, 2025
entry.summary: «I am in the enviable position where I’m building a game for work! The goal is to make the conversational experience we’re creating feel more engaging, so the idea was to build a memory palace of the…»

entry.title: Weeknote #12 [W25.51] - A week of watching films in Trivandrum
entry.summary: «I spent most of last week in Trivandrum, watching films, and having dinner with Manu afterwards. I also met my friend and ex-colleague Arjun a few times, who was attending the festival too.»

entry.summary: «I have a new home for my blog at blog.anikthink.com»

entry.title: [Weeknotes] Week of Dec 13th, 2025
entry.summary: «Video generation is expensive! What is the stack people typically work with, how do they prompt their models, how do they store and access memories for those models, and how do they not burn hundreds…»

entry.title: Weeknote #10 [W25.49] - First screening of Cinema Next Door, and why I should stop drinking coffee
entry.summary: «We finally had our first screening of Cinema Next Door last Tuesday on Dec 2. We screened Satyajit Ray’s Pather Panchali.»

entry.title: Weekly Notes - Week 1 - Zero banana-liking dogs found
entry.summary: «I’ve started writing weekly notes after being inspired by Ankur, Tanvi, and Abhinav at my first-ever IndieWeb Club meetup in Bangalore a few weeks ago. While I won’t be able to share updates about my…»

entry.title: Weeknotes: Week 1 Post-retreat life
entry.summary: «I am writing Weeknotes as part of the Indiewebclub #11 prompt. The week was full of learnings : ) [Friday] We celebrated a friend’s birthday, one of the gifts was a printed tee, and I was happy using…»

entry.title: Weeknote: 04 October 2025
entry.summary: «My personal celebration this week is that I did released the OCaml handlebars templating library that I was working on. I felt happy when I read the "Thanks" and "Your PR ... has been merged" emails…»

entry.title: Weeknote #1 [W25.40]: Writing in public, reflections on breaking up, and thoughts on structures
entry.summary: «I’m writing this post at a meeting of the Indie Web Club at Underline Center. The last time I had a public blog was around 12 years ago. I have written things then â€” journals, essays, and the like…»

entry.title: Notes for the Week #40 (2025)
